name: Alto Bernesga and Los Argüellos Biospheres Loop
name_original: Reserva de la biosfera de Los Argüellos - circular por Embalse de Casares desde Villamanín
description: Cycle a difficult 66-mile route through the Reserva de la Biosfera de Los Argüellos, featuring 5836 feet of climbing and views of Embalse de
region_intro: Road cycling around Villamanín, located in the province of León, Spain, features a diverse landscape ideal for cyclists. The region is characterized by dramatic gorges, verdant valleys, and challenging mountain passes. Cyclists can navigate routes through areas like the Los Calderones Gorge, the Vegacervera Gorge, and the Valle de La Tercia, which offer varied terrain from well-paved roads to more demanding ascents. The area's mountainous nature includes significant climbs, providing a range of experiences for different skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Villamanín The most popular road cycling route is Barrios de Luna Reservoir and La Robla Loop, a 46.8 miles (75.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Barrios de Luna Reservoir and covers varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto Bernesga and Los Argüellos Biospheres Loop, a difficult 66.0 miles (106.2 km) path. This route traverses two biosphere reserves, featuring significant elevation gain and expansive natural scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Convento de San Marcos – Ermita del Buen Suceso loop from Pola de Gordón, a 44.6 miles (71.7 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past historical sites, often completed in about 2 hours 58 minutes. Road cycling around Villamanín is defined by challenging mountain passes, spectacular gorges, and tranquil valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult climbs. The routes in Villamanín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villamanín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villamanín?
A: Villamanín offers a diverse selection of nearly 20 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from moderate to difficult, ensuring there's a suitable option for most cyclists.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Villamanín?
A: Yes, Villamanín is known for its challenging terrain, featuring significant mountain passes and demanding ascents. Approximately half of the routes are classified as difficult, providing a rigorous experience for advanced cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042304517/alto-bernesga-and-los-argueellos-biospheres-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Bernesga and Los Argüellos Biospheres Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 106.2 km path with substantial elevation gain.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or natural attractions can I see along road cycling routes in Villamanín?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through impressive geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3541942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vegacervera Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, which offer spectacular panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt; also provides a beautiful natural setting. Many routes traverse the Valle de La Tercia, known for its pure natural beauty with green fields, mountains, and rivers.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly road cycling routes in Villamanín?
A: While many routes in Villamanín are moderate to difficult, the region does offer some easier sections, particularly within areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt;, which are noted for providing easy routes suitable for families. However, dedicated easy road cycling routes are less common compared to the more challenging options.

Q: What do other road cyclists think about the routes in Villamanín?
A: The road cycling routes in Villamanín are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, challenging climbs, and tranquil valleys that define the region's cycling experience.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Villamanín?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Villamanín are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042316978/barrios-de-luna-reservoir-and-la-robla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrios de Luna Reservoir and La Robla Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31861029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Convento de San Marcos – Ermita del Buen Suceso loop from Pola de Gordón&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied scenery and experiences.

Q: Which routes explore the dramatic gorges and valleys of Villamanín?
A: Several routes allow you to experience the region's impressive gorges and valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31856213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pola de Gordón Town Center – Vegacervera Gorge loop from Pola de Gordón&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes you through the stunning Vegacervera Gorge. Other routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042304517/alto-bernesga-and-los-argueellos-biospheres-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Bernesga and Los Argüellos Biospheres Loop&lt;/a&gt;, traverse areas rich in geological formations and verdant valleys.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villamanín?
A: The mountainous nature of Villamanín suggests that late spring, summer, and early autumn are generally the most favorable times for road cycling. During these periods, the weather is typically milder, and mountain passes are more accessible, offering the best conditions to enjoy the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes in Villamanín that pass by historical sites?
A: Yes, some routes offer a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31861029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Convento de San Marcos – Ermita del Buen Suceso loop from Pola de Gordón&lt;/a&gt; leads through rural landscapes and past historical sites, including the Hermitage of the Virgin of Manadero, located inside the Cueva de las Palomas.

Q: Can I find less crowded road cycling routes in Villamanín?
A: While specific crowd levels can vary, the extensive network of routes through natural reserves and less populated areas often allows for a more tranquil cycling experience. Exploring routes that venture deeper into the Valle de La Tercia or the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve can offer a sense of solitude amidst nature.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that feature reservoirs in Villamanín?
A: Yes, the region includes routes that offer views of impressive reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042316978/barrios-de-luna-reservoir-and-la-robla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrios de Luna Reservoir and La Robla Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, providing scenic vistas of the Barrios de Luna Reservoir as you cycle.
tips: Villamanín is a small town in the La Tercia region on the N-630 national highway. Near the source of the Bernesga river on the valley route between León and Gijón.
Ciñera is a typical small industrial mining town in the mountain valley. Located on the Bernesga river.
Valdepiélago is a small town next to the Curueño river and the entrance to the Curueño gorge. Spectacular views of the jagged edge mountain rock formation as the road angles along the river gorge.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging racebike route is approximately 106 km long with significant elevation gain. It typically takes around 5 hours and 20 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on your fitness level and pace.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult for racebiking. It involves substantial ascents and descents, requiring very good fitness due to the mountainous terrain of the Reserva de la Biosfera de Los Argüellos.

faqs.question: Where does this circular cycling route start and end?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one, starting and ending in Villamanín. This allows for convenient access and return to your starting point.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: You can expect mostly paved surfaces, typical for road cycling, though some sections might be less maintained. The terrain is characterized by the Cantabrian mountain ecosystem, featuring karstic landscapes, high mountain pastures, and rocky areas, which contribute to the significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: What scenic highlights or viewpoints will I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking scenery within the UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Key points of interest include the picturesque Embalse de Casares (Casares Reservoir), and you'll pass through Villamanín. You might also see the Plaza Mayor of Villamanín, the Church of Ciñera, and the Curueño Gorge and Valdepiélago Bridges.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see in the Reserva de la Biosfera de Los Argüellos?
faqs.answer: The Biosphere Reserve is rich in biodiversity. Keen observers might spot Cantabrian brown bears, wolves, otters, wildcats, chamois, golden eagles, griffon vultures, and falcons, among other species.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time for road cycling in the Reserva de la Biosfera de Los Argüellos is generally during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the weather is more stable. Summer can also be suitable, but be prepared for potentially higher temperatures, especially in the valleys.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Reserva de la Biosfera de Los Argüellos?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are generally required for cycling on public roads within the Reserva de la Biosfera de Los Argüellos. However, always respect local regulations and protected areas.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for cycling with dogs?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebiking on roads, which is generally not suitable for cycling with dogs, especially given the distance and difficulty. If you wish to bring a dog, consider shorter, dedicated walking or mountain biking trails where dogs are permitted and can safely accompany you.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car near the starting point in Villamanín?
faqs.answer: Villamanín serves as the starting and ending point for this circular route. You should be able to find parking options within the town, particularly near the Plaza Mayor of Villamanín or other public areas, though specific cyclist parking facilities may vary.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other official trails. You will briefly pass through parts of the Camino Olvidado (Puente Almuhey-La Robla), Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón, Vía Verde Mina Bardaya, Camino del Salvador, and Vía de la Plata.
